name: ExtensionMeasure_Group_Subject
title: R5: E.g. Patient, Practitioner, RelatedPerson, Organization, Location, Device (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Measure.group.subject[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Measure.group.subject[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Measure.group.subject[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Measure.group.subject[x]` has a context of Measure.group based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.subject[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: E.g. Patient, Practitioner, RelatedPerson, Organization, Location, Device (new)
element.comment:
Element `Measure.group.subject[x]` has a context of Measure.group based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.subject[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The subject of the measure is critical in interpreting the criteria definitions, as the logic in the measures is evaluated with respect to a particular subject. This corresponds roughly to the notion of a Compartment in that it limits what content is available based on its relationship to the subject. In CQL, this corresponds to the context declaration.

element.short: E.g. Patient, Practitioner, RelatedPerson, Organization, Location, Device
element.definition: The intended subjects for the measure. If this element is not provided, a Patient subject is assumed, but the subject of the measure can be anything.
element.comment: The subject of the measure is critical in interpreting the criteria definitions, as the logic in the measures is evaluated with respect to a particular subject. This corresponds roughly to the notion of a Compartment in that it limits what content is available based on its relationship to the subject. In CQL, this corresponds to the context declaration.

binding.description: The possible types of subjects for a measure (E.g. Patient, Practitioner, Organization, Location, etc.).
